caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is supplied at five-digit 56173 rather than six-digit 561730.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Autonomous mower adoption is not counted as AI task replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: BrightView is a public commercial-scale comparator, not proof of small-target contract terms.
inputs.pi_moat: Platform concentration evidence does not establish moat for a single local target.
inputs.t50_years: Trade-survey technology context is not a measured AI penetration series.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No code-specific, representative AI-adoption percentage was located in the reopened source.
inputs.historical_analogs: The source is trade-survey context rather than an outcome study.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not an observed landscaping-owner statistic; it should not be treated as succession evidence.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Workforce scarcity is not interchangeable with owner succession.
inputs.active_consolidators: Ranked firms are not all active acquirers in every local market.
inputs.buy_mult: S5 is SDE and mostly far below the stated target band; S6 is a non-transaction-level compilation. Verify actual LOIs before underwriting.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit depends on recurring maintenance mix, management depth and buyer-market conditions; no disclosed band-size transaction series supports a precise spread.
inputs.pricing_structure: Public and LM150 sources overrepresent larger commercial operators.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[56]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $152,919 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 561730); class <5: 82,695 firms, avg revenue/firm $299,970 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $46,945 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 16,643 firms, avg revenue/firm $933,160 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $146,040 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 8,936 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,879,235 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $294,100 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 5,888 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,038,071 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $788,458 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 593 firms, avg revenue/firm $22,899,949 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,583,842 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 87 firms, avg revenue/firm $206,781,494 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $32,361,304 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 593

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI materially changes office, bookkeeping, estimating and selected management tasks, but the wage base is overwhelmingly physical crews and supervisors. Robotics is excluded from this labor-task estimate.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 561730 ($45,367,770,541) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 561730 ($118,363,300,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

sector.name: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. Reopened sources support the modest-AI, physical-work and fragmented-market thesis, and no factor semantics or capture/terminal double counting is reversed. The record remains useful as a kill conclusion, but its adoption, consolidator, capture and multiple inputs rely on explicitly disclosed estimates and broad comparators, so the authored caveats must remain visible.

sources.title: BrightView Holdings, Inc. 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial landscaping maintenance revenue, 13.0% segment adjusted EBITDA margin, fragmentation and recurring-demand context.

sources.title: LM150: 2025 Top 25
sources.what_it_supports: Named scaled landscaping maintenance and commercial platforms and their reported revenue ranking.

sources.title: Lawn & Landscape 2024 Technology Report
sources.what_it_supports: Early industry technology context and the separation of AI discussion from autonomous/robotic mower adoption.

sources.title: NALP 2023 Business Pulse Survey
sources.what_it_supports: Industry respondent evidence that staffing is a leading business challenge and that maintenance-focused firms differ from other respondents.

sources.title: BizBuySell Landscaping & Yard Service Valuation Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: 2021-2025 sold small-business SDE and revenue multiple distribution; this is below the intended EBITDA band and is used only as a lower-scale anchor.

sources.title: EBITDA Multiples for Landscaping Companies: 2025 Report
sources.what_it_supports: Third-party Q1 2025 private-landscaping EBITDA indications by commercial, full-service, lawn and residential subcategory; methodology is not transaction-level and is a caveated upper anchor.

title: Landscaping Services
